content: Which of the following languages belongs to the Indo-European family?
Which of the following is the second largest language family in the world?
Which language branch does English belong to?
Which language has the closest relationship with English in terms of origin?
As the most convenient tool of human communication, in what way can the communicative function of language come true?
RP, short for _______ Pronunciation, is the instantly recognizable accent often described as ‘typically British’.
The organs that take part in the production of speech sounds are called _____.
Professor A.C. Gimson once noted... The statement underlines _______.
options: D. the equal importance of pronunciation, vocabulary and grammar

content: Using the diacritics provided by the IPA, _______ transcription captures as many aspects of a specific pronunciation as possible.
English phonemes are divided into vowels and consonants. The distinction between the two lies in __________.
options: C. the vibration of vocal cords

content: What are the fundamental elements of a language?
Which of the following expressions equal(s) to lingua franca?
What is the most commonly used model accent for learners of English as a foreign or second language?
The pronunciation of a language consists of three basic elements: _________.
Languages only exist in the community of humans.
It is possible to communicate effectively among speakers of different English varieties.
Both RP and GA are widely used by well-educated people and on national radios and TV networks.
Both broad transcription and narrow transcription are used to transcribe or document utterances in ordinary English learning.
Broad transcription describes only the most noticeable phonetic features of an utterance.
A lingua franca is always an official language of the countries where it is used.
